member.name: F:PriorityQueue.PriorityQueue`1.nextIndex
member.summary: The next index to be used for insertion.

member.name: F:PriorityQueue.PriorityQueue`1.data
member.summary: The backing array actually containing all of our beautiful data

member.name: M:PriorityQueue.PriorityQueue`1.Trickle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Restore heap property, which might be violated by data.[index] being smaller that its "children"

member.name: M:PriorityQueue.PriorityQueue`1.RemoveWhere(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{`0,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Remove all elements of the priority queue for which pred holds

member.name: M:PriorityQueue.PriorityQueue`1.Heapify(System.Int32)
member.summary: Restore heap property, which might be violated by data.[index] being larger than its "parent"

member.name: T:PriorityQueue.PriorityQueue`1
member.summary: A priority queue backed by an array-based binary heap. Larger priorities come first.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.MaybeMonad
member.summary: Maybe monad. Lightweight exception-like handling (.NET exceptions are too expensive)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.timeout(System.Double)
member.summary: Set/reset the T2 timeout mechanism

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.check_timeout
member.summary: Call to see if timeout threshold has been exceeded

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.Set`1.addAll.Static``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{``1},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``1})
member.summary: Returns the set s1 \cup set_of(s2)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.Set`1.removeAll.Static``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{``1},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``1})
member.summary: Returns the set s1 \setminus set_of(s2)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.memoize``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,``1})
member.summary: Make a cached-version of f

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.die``1
member.summary: Uses .NET tricks to find out the location from which die has been called.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.dieWith``1(System.String)
member.summary: Uses .NET tricks to find out the location from which dieWith has been called.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.true_string(System.String)
member.summary: Return true if the input looks like its saying "yes/true/etc"

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.run_clear
member.summary: Trigger an "end event"

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.add_clear(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit})
member.summary: Add a function to be called at an "end event"

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.clear_functions
member.summary: List of functions that should be called when T2 is ending some reasoning and moving to a new problem. Caches, Gensym, etc can add reset functions here. Use the "add_clear" function

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.create_defect_files
member.summary: When testing we dont want to create a billion defect files, so we can shut this option off by setting create_defect_files to false

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.seq_interpolation
member.summary: Do efficient sequential interpolation (generating all interpolants for a path in one go) instead of standard interpolation

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.seq_interpolation_ignore_last_constr
member.summary: Try to ignore constraints from the beginning

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.check_interpolants
member.summary: Double check interpolants found

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.print_interpolants
member.summary: Print interpolants found

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.chaining
member.summary: Chain transitions that connect via unlabeled nodes.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.iterative_reachability
member.summary: Do iterative reachability proving, where only a part of the graph needs to be recomputed after changes:

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.fc_unsat_core
member.summary: Use UNSAT core to reduce interpolation constraints

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.fc_remove_on_fail
member.summary: When trying force-cover v by w fails, remove w from the candidates of v

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.fc_look_back
member.summary: Use path from root when doing force-cover

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.safety_counterexample
member.summary: Produce safety counterexamples

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.sanity_checking
member.summary: Check sanity of Impact graphs

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.dottify_input_pgms
member.summary: Generate DOT graphs of the input program

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.dottify_reachability
member.summary: Generate DOT graphs of the impact tree (at each step)

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.safety_implementation
member.summary: Type of used safety prover.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.mcnp_style_bound_decr
member.summary: Should we use bounded/decreasing split from Lemma 31 in "SAT-Based Termination Analysis Using Monotonicity Constraints over the Integers" (TPLP)

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.lex_term_proof_first
member.summary: Try lexicographic termination proofs with program simplifications before starting safety proof?

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.polyrank
member.summary: Start looking for polyranking lexicographic rank functions in the event of failure with lexicographic RFs. Does nothing if lexicographic isn't on.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.lex_opt_scc_unaffected
member.summary: Try to find a rf such that the considered relation is strictly decreasing and bounded and all SCC transitions are weakly decreasing

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.lex_opt_max_unaffect
member.summary: Try to find lexicographic RFs s.t. the relations affect each other's RFs as little as possible? Don't have this on at the same time as fewer_rfs

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.lex_opt_fewer_rfs
member.summary: Try to find lexicographic RFs of shorter length? Don't have this on at the same time as max_unaffect

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.lexicographic
member.summary: Try to find lexicographic rank functions for all cutpoints

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.unrolling
member.summary: If lexicographic fails, try unrolling a bit?

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.init_cond
member.summary: If lexicographic fails, try detecting initial condition?

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.prove_nonterm
member.summary: Try proving non-termination as well as termination?

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.ai_domain
member.summary: Choice of numerical domain for abstract interpretation

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.did_ai_first
member.summary: This is flipped to true if we performed AI and enables further optimizations later on

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.do_ai_threshold
member.summary: Perform numerical abstr. interpretation if we have less than !do_ai_threshold transitions

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.elim_temp_vars
member.summary: Propagates assignments to variables through the program as far as possible. Very helpful to eliminate temporary variables introduced by SSA transformations (i.e. when examples are coming from LLVM).

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.elim_constants
member.summary: Symbolic abstraction: change constants in the program from 5 to var5. Constraints are added so that var5<var6,etc. This is needed because otherwise, the reachability graph is unwound (i.e., the program is executed!) until actually reaching the constants. Not so bad for 5, but very bad for 1000.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.print_debug
member.summary: Set to true to enable verbose debug logging spew from across T2

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.print_log
member.summary: Set to true to enable verbose logging spew from across T2

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.print_stats
member.summary: Print some statistics about our efforts, after finishing

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.print_proof
member.summary: Print ranking functions / recurrence sets to stdout after the proof?

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.timeout
member.summary: Overall timeout per program (in seconds):

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters.start_time
member.summary: Time when T2 execution began

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Stats.printStatistics
member.summary: Print out statistics

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Stats.incCounter(System.String)
member.summary: Increment counter s

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Stats.addToCounter(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Add x to the counter s

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Stats.endTimer(System.String)
member.summary: Note end time of some timer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Stats.startTimer(System.String)
member.summary: Record start time of some timer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Test.register_test(System.Boolean,System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,System.Boolean})
member.summary: First argument is expected result. Conventionally correct test should always return true. If test is predictably failing for some reason (for instance, some feature is not implemented), this value is set to false. So if this feature eventually was implemented, it would show up as regression, and thus attract developer's attention.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Test.register_testd(System.Boolean,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,System.Boolean})
member.summary: if "register_testd" gets called we'll shut off normal testing and just run the one case we're debugging ..........this allows you to easily debug a broken regression test by swapping a call to "register_test" with "register_testd"

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Test.run_tests(System.Double)
member.summary: run_tests should be called once register_test has been called on all of the desired unit tests

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Test.found_debug
member.summary: This is set to true if we use register_testd instead of register_test. The behavior of T2 changes radically if this is set to true. We are no longer testing, but debugging a test

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Dominators.well_formed(Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.DefaultDictionary{System.Int32,System.Int32},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.Int32})
member.summary: Are all elements of the set dominated by one element? Equivalenthly, does set have single entry point?

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Dominators.dominates(Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.DefaultDictionary{System.Int32,System.Int32},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Return true if a dominates b and false otherwise

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Dominators.find_dominators``3(Microsoft.Research.T2.Utils.ListDictionary{System.Int32,System.Tuple{``0,System.Tuple{``1,``2,System.Int32}}},System.Int32)
member.summary: Compute dominator tree, following the sophisticated method from Lengauer & Tarjan '79:

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Z.print_sat_model``1(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Z3.BoolExpr},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{``0})
member.summary: SAT-solve and then print the model. Useful for debugging.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Z.simplify(Microsoft.Z3.Expr)
member.summary: Z3 term simplificaton

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Z.internalSolve(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.UInt32},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Z3.BoolExpr})
member.summary:
Satisfy as much formulae in the beginning of the list as possible. If list is empty or first formula is UNSAT, return None.
Typical use is to call it with a list [f, h1, h2...], where f is formula we absolutely want to satisfy, and h1, h2... are heuristics we'd like to satisfy if possible.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Z.solve_k(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.UInt32},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Z3.BoolExpr})
member.summary:
Return Some(k, model) for largest k such that formulae 0..k are SAT. In case such k does not exist (that is, should be -1), return None.
Typical use is to call it with a list [f, h1, h2...], where f is formula we absolutely want to satisfy, and h1, h2... are heuristics we'd like to satisfy if possible.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Z.getSolver(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.UInt32})
member.summary: Create a simple z3 SMT Solver. Caller has to take care of disposal of the object.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Z.z3Context
member.summary: The actual z3 context through we which pipe all of our work. It should be disposed at the end of a run.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Var.get_prime_idx(System.String)
member.summary: Assuming that SSA information is after ^

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Var.prime_var(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Encoding SSA using numbers after "^"

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Var.unprime_var(System.String)
member.summary: Assuming that SSA information is after ^

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.alpha(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,System.String},Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term)
member.summary: Alpha-renaming

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.fromZ3result
member.summary: Used internally. Since Z3 formulas do not distinguish between "formula" and "term", it's convenient to have a union of these two types during translation.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.fromZ3(Microsoft.Z3.Expr)
member.summary: Translate a Z3.Term to a Formula.formula.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.z3f(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.fromZ3result)
member.summary: Get the formula from the union

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.z3t(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.fromZ3result)
member.summary: Get the term from the union

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.eval_const_var(System.String)
member.summary: For __const_42 return Term.Const(42). To use in subst

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.unsat(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Satisfiability procedure for T2 formulae

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.entails(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula,Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Entailment procedure for T2 formulae

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.valid
member.summary: Z3-based validity procedure for T2 formulae

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.z3(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Convert T2 Formula to Z3 Formula

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.polyhedra_dnf(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Convert to DNF where atomic inequalities are of the form t1 <= t2 (they are supposed to be linear, but it is not checked here) This is a preferred way of simplifying formulae. It's supposed to be fast: O(size of result).

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.split_disjunction(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Break a formulae into a list of its disjuncts

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.split_conjunction(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Break a formulae into a list of its conjuncts

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.alpha(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,System.String},Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Alpha-renaming

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.Z3_op_not_supported
member.summary: Raise this if we can't translate a Z3 formula back to Formula.formula

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.SparseLinear.term_as_linear
member.summary: ensure that term is linear and simplify it a little bit. If it's not linear, Nonlinear exception is thrown

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.SparseLinear.eliminate_var(System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger}})
member.summary: Fourier-Motzkin elimination. Terms are understood as system of inequalities t <= 0. (since we can't represent congruence module constant, it's imprecise (consider elimination of y from x <= 2*y && x >= 2*y))

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.SparseLinear.formula_to_linear_terms(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Return list ts of linear terms t such that conjunction of t<=0 is equivalent to given formula. Formula is expected to be a conjunction of atomic equalities and inequalities.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.SparseLinear.simplify_as_inequalities(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger}})
member.summary: Simplify conjunction of inequalities represented by terms (as t <= 0)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.SparseLinear.simplify_as_inequality(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger})
member.summary: divide by gcd

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.SparseLinear.ONE
member.summary: special 'variable' name to represent constants

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation
member.summary: (formula, prevars, postvars)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.standardise_postvars(Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation)
member.summary: Takes in a relation, and replaces the postvar indices with "post" throughout

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.existentials(Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation)
member.summary: List of additional variables mentioned in the relation formula

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSequence.synthesis(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,System.Boolean,System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula})
member.summary: Compute path interpolants
try to find interpolant such that v |= w, where v is the last interpolant and w is 'entail_distance' from last if one inequality interpolation does not work we try to add invariants to support it

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSequence.synthesis_base(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,System.Boolean,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger}}})
member.summary: Compute path interpolants.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSequence.gen_system(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger}}})
member.summary: Generate interpolant constraints for a list of systems S, each system is of form A<=0. Return (common constraints, constraints for non-strict case, interpolants, heuristics constraints). Interpolants should be understood as intp<=0. try_ignore_beginning: if true, the heuristics constraints will be a list of constraints enforcing the first lambdas to 0 (i.e. ignore the first nodes on the path)

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSequence.number_of_invars
member.summary: number_of_invars:

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSequence.number_of_implications
member.summary: number_of_implications: when all_implications is set to false this variable determines how many implications to generate.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSequence.all_implications
member.summary: all_implications: if set to true we generate constraints for all backwards implications. Otherwise see number_of_implications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSingle.synthesis(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula})
member.summary: Compute path interpolants

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSingle.synthesis_base(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula,Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Return interpolant between two formulas

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.InterpolantSingle.gen_system(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger}},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger}})
member.summary: Generate interpolant constraints for systems A<=0, B<=0. Return pair (constraints, intp). Interpolant should be understood as intp<=0.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.incompleteAbstraction
member.summary: Flag indicating that we abstracted away things in an incomplete manner and hence should not report non-term

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.usedConstants
member.summary: Constants used in the program.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.locations
member.summary: Locations in the program

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.variables
member.summary: Variables in the program

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.activeTransitions
member.summary: x \in active iff transitions[x] != (-1,_,-1)
take function that transforms transition and apply it to every transition in the program

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.RemoveUnreachableLocations
member.summary: Prune out unreachable locations in the program

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.RemoveTransition(System.Int32)
member.summary: Remove transition with index idx from the program

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.LetConvert(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.String}})
member.summary: If variables are used temporarly in basic blocks and then are killed or not used elsewhere, LetConvert removes them in the obvious way. livevars is assumed to be the set of live variables Our heuristic is based on the idea that more variables are bad, given how constraint-based rank function synthesis and interpolation work. It also special cases instrumentation variables, which might be considered dead, but are still important to us.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.GetTransitiveCFG
member.summary: Return a mapping from nodes to nodes that are reachable using the CFG edges

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.GetSCCSGs
member.summary: Returns strongly connected subgraphs in the transitive closure of the program's control-flow graph.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.GetLabelledNode(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the node in the program with the given label. If no such node existed, creates a new one.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.GetIsolatedRegions
member.summary:
For each node we return a set of nodes representing the nodes "inside" the loop. As described in "Variance analyses from invariance analyses", POPL'07
Return list of pairs (cutpoint, corresponding region)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.GetDominatorsFrom(System.Int32)
member.summary: Compute dominators tree

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.Clone
member.summary: Return deep copy of program

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.ChainTransitions(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Int32},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Chain linear sequences of program transitions, in-place. Returns a map from original transition IDs to the freshly chosen transition IDs.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.AddTransitionMapped(System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command},System.String)
member.summary: add transition n--T-->M with preprocessing (eliminates constants, creates DNF)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.AddTransition(System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command},System.Int32)
member.summary: add transition source -- cmds --> target as it is, without any preprocessing

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program.AddSymbolConstantInformation
member.summary: Adds a new initial transition that asserts relative information on symbolized constants (e.g., __const_4 < __const_10)

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program
member.summary: Default size for transitions array

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command.Assume
member.summary: Assume(p,f) represents "if (f) ignore" at location p

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command.Assign
member.summary: Assign(p,v,t) represents v <- t at location p

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command
member.summary: Commands datatype

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.pos
member.summary: File position information for commands: (line number, file name)
Merge chains of transitions together.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.const_subst(Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command)
member.summary: Replace __const variables by the actual constants again

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.collapse_path``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Tuple{System.Int32,``0,System.Int32}})
member.summary: Collapse adjacent commands so that if there were multiple commands in the transition from location x to location y, we end up with a single list of commands for the transition from x to y. Example: [(1, a, 2), (1, b, 2), (2, c, 3), (2, d, 3)] -> [(1, [a, b], 2), (2, [c, d], 3)] Loop edges are not allowed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.commands_constants(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.Numerics.BigInteger},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command})
member.summary: Replace all constants that are not in range with special variables like __const__42. Return new commands and set of eliminated constants.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.formula_constants(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.Numerics.BigInteger},Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Replace all constants that are not in range with special variables like __const__42. Return new formula and set of eliminated constants.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.term_constants(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.Numerics.BigInteger},Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term)
member.summary: Replace all constants that are not in 'range' with special variables like __const__42. Return new term and set of eliminated constants.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.modified(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command})
member.summary: Return the set of variables modified by a sequence of commands

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.freevars(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command})
member.summary: Return free variables in a sequence of commands

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.bound_constants
member.summary: Constants not abstracted away because they are used as bounds

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Symex.path_to_relation``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Tuple{``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command},``1}},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.String})
member.summary: Vars from 'vars' go to prevars and postvars. Prevars and postvars are guaranteed to be distinct.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Symex.path_to_transitions_and_var_map``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Tuple{``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command},``1}},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.Int32})
member.summary: Convert path to path formula, applying SSA transformation. Var_map contains var to SSA version number. Return pair (<list of transition formulae>, <new var_map>). If variable is not present in var_map, its version is 0.

Remove unneeded statements from path pi (assignments to variables that never influence assumes) Second parameter gives commands that are relevant for the "needed" bit, but are not supposed to be cleaned

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.dbm
M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.widen(Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct)
member.summary: inplace widen; return true if widening changed the octagon

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.var_index(System.String)
M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.union(Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct)
member.summary: inplace union

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.to_formula_filtered(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,System.Boolean})
member.summary: return conjunction of (linear_termt1 <= linear_term2)

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.size
member.summary: number of variables (not the size of dbm!)

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.is_empty
member.summary: Test is precise only immediately after tight_closure. Otherwise false negatives are possible.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.check
member.summary: check coherence and some other internal invariants

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.delete_var(System.String)
member.summary: Projection

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.conservative_assume(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: does not introduce variables that are not already in the octagon; potentially O(n^3) in the size of formula

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.assume(Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: may introduce new variables from f to octagon

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.Oct.assign(System.String,Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term)
P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.sanity_check
member.summary: internal Oct invariants checks; somewhat expensive

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Octagon2.floor_div(System.Numerics.BigInteger,System.Numerics.BigInteger)
member.summary: div that rounds down even for negative x

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.IntervalIntDom.Intervals.vars
member.summary: Variables we know something about. Everything that's not in there has a completely unknown value

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.IntervalIntDom.Intervals.widen(Microsoft.Research.T2.IntervalIntDom.Intervals)
member.summary: The other is always the "newer" value (obtained lated in the program):

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Counterexample.cex
member.summary: Counterexample to safety property ---> cycle = None. Counterexample to liveness property ---> stem might be equal to None.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Counterexample.print_defect(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Counterexample.cex},System.String)
member.summary: Print SDV defect tool input in "fname"

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.dead
member.summary: Nodes that were in the system but have now been dead and GC'd

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.garbage
member.summary: Nodes that have been deemed as garbage but havent been GC'd yet.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.stack
member.summary: The priority queue used during DFS.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.dfs_cnt
member.summary: Helper to create dfs numbers

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.dfs_map
member.summary: Node numbers do not quite represent the order that we've "visited" them. dfs_map maps each node number to its dfs number

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.fc_candidates
member.summary: Per-node list of candidates that we should search for when looking for a force cover

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.covering
member.summary: Induction coverings

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.program_loc_to_abs_nodes
member.summary: A node n\in representatives(k) if n in the graph maps back to k in the original program. i.e. if n\in representatives(k) then node_map n = k

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.abs_node_to_program_loc
member.summary: Mapping from nodes in the execution tree to the original program

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.abs_edge_to_program_commands
member.summary: Mapping from edges in the execution tree to the commands in the original program

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.leaves
member.summary: Current leaves in the execution tree

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.psi@120
member.summary: Set of interpolants (understood as conjunction) for each node

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.parent@117
member.summary: parents in the graph

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.E
member.summary: Edge relation

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.V
member.summary: Current nodes in the system

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.cnt
member.summary: Tree/Graph constructon representing the state of the safety proof procedure. Back edges in the "covering" relations represent induction checks. At the end of a successful proof search this graph should represent a valid proof of safety which could be spit out to a theorem prover. Note that for performance there is A LOT of redundency in this graph. Its RIDDLED with invariants. Fun. Counter tracking how many nodes we have in the system

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.priority
member.summary: Priority of each node in the original program. Used to choose which node to take during DFS

member.name: F:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.loc_init
member.summary: Initial program location

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.try_force_cover(System.Int32)
member.summary: Apply force_cover procedure as appropriate to the nodes in the graph.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.sq_eq(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: x is y, parent of y, grandparent of y, etc.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.sq(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: x is parent of y, grandparent of y, etc.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.refine(System.Int32)
member.summary: If an error path was spurious, refine abstraction and return None. Otherwise, return true (almost) error path.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.pick_vertex
member.summary: pick uncovered leaf that a) preferably corresponds to error loc b) is earliest in dfs order

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.make_node(System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command},System.Int32)
member.summary: Make a new node "w" and add the edge expandedNode---commandsOnEdge--->newNode, where newNode maps to location newNodeLoc from the original program

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.is_covered(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if the node is covered by an induction covering, or if its parent is.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.get_force_cover_candidates(System.Int32)
member.summary: This computes candidates for coverings (it uses fc_candidates to cache results -- the set of candidates can never grow, as we are only considering things that were computed before)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.gc
member.summary: Garbage collection procedure. Roots of trees become garbage when their interpolant becomes "false". The procedure for strengthening interpolants checks for unsat and puts nodes in garbage when this condition is met.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.force_cover(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: If cover doesnt work, we can try to force it to work by (in a sense) merging paths and finding necessary interpolants along the way

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.find_path_from(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Find the path from node x to node y in the tree, given that x sqeq y

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.find_path(System.Int32)
member.summary: Find the path from the root of the tree to x

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.expand(System.Int32)
member.summary: Expand the graph for DFS search

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.dfs(System.Int32)
member.summary: Depth first search: in this procedure we start building the tree in a DFS manner until we hit what we think might be an error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.delete_tree(System.Int32)
member.summary: Procedure for removing from the graph. I'm being pretty aggressive right now because in early days I was seeing lots of performance bugs due to reasoning about dead nodes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.db
member.summary: dump abstraction graph to file for debugging

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.cover(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Try to cover v with w. That is, v entails w, where v is later in the execution and the cover represents the induction check

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.conjoin_with_psi(System.Int32,Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula)
member.summary: Strengthen the invariant at a node.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.close_all_ancestors(System.Int32)
member.summary: Try to find coverings on all of the nodes above v

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.close(System.Int32)
member.summary: This procedure attempts to find a cover for v.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.check_not_removed(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sanity check to be used before using a node

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.ancestors(System.Int32)
member.summary: Compute ancestors

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.Microsoft-Research-T2-SafetyInterface-SafetyProver-ResetFrom(System.Int32)
member.summary: For incrementality, we sometimes need to delete a subtree within the proof graph

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.Microsoft-Research-T2-SafetyInterface-SafetyProver-ErrorLocationReachable
member.summary: Return path to loc_err or None if it's unreachable

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.ImpactARG.Microsoft-Research-T2-SafetyInterface-SafetyProver-DeleteProgramTransition(System.Tuple{System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command},System.Int32})
member.summary: Delete everything from the graph that used/used some program transition:

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.gc_check
member.summary: Should we perform some sanity checks to make sure that we're not using nodes that have been GC'd?

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Impact.do_gc
member.summary: Should we perform garbage collection on the abstraction graph?

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.MuZ.printSMT2Horn
member.summary: Debug helper. If true, prints generated clauses on STDOUT.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Safety.prover(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program,System.Int32)
member.summary: Prove that location err is unreachable in p

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.synthesis_poly_lex(Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation},System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to find a lexicographic polyranking function with rel_to_add inserted into partial_order. Returns None or a list of Lex_Poly_RF. Note partial_order will be empty initially

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.make_eventually_neg_constraints(Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.template_tree,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Int32})
member.summary: Makes constraint that says the rf at the head of the tree is Eventually Negative by A in T_bar

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.make_unaffecting_constraint_consts(Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,Microsoft.Z3.ArithExpr})
member.summary: Generates the constraint that rf is unaffected by rel. rf contains its own constant

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.make_bounded_constraints_consts(Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,Microsoft.Z3.ArithExpr})
member.summary: Generates constraint that rf is bounded below by zero on rel. rf contains its own constant

Given a list of transition as (transitionIdx, fromPC, relation, toPC), this tries to find a lexicographic ranking function that maximizes the number of strictly decreasing and bound transitions and ensures that all others are unaffected (corresponding to the DepPair approach). It returns a list of the used rank functions and bounds, and a set of transitions that were strictly oriented, allowing to remove them from the instrumented program copy.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.synthesis_lex(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program,System.Int32,Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation})
member.summary: Tries to find a lexicographic ranking function with rel_to_add inserted into partial_order. Returns None or a list of Lex_RF. Note partial_order will be empty initially
Try to find a lex rf such that all SCC transitions are unaffected and the cex is decreasing. Then find out what part of the SCC might be deleted.
For each possible insertion of rel_to_add to partial_order, try to find a lexicographic solution. Minimize the number of used rfs in that.
For each possible insertion of rel_to_add to partial_order, try to find a lexicographic solution. Order results such that solutions with more unaffected relations are preferred
For each possible insertion of rel_to_add to partial_order, try to find a lexicographic solution

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.combs``1(System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{``0})
member.summary: Gives a list of all selections of k objects from input_list Assumes there are no duplicates in input_list
Constructs contraints such that all prior rfs mu.[j] orient rel_i weakly and relation i is bounded and strictly decreasing using mu.[i]

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.construct_mu(System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Int32},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: construct mu_k for every program point k, where every prevar gets a coefficient, for a number of lex ranking functions:

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.synthesis(Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation)
member.summary: Return pair (rf, bound), where ref is term in prevars, and bound is consant term. RF should be always >= bound.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.extract_rf_from_model``3(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{``1,``2}},Microsoft.Z3.Model)
member.summary: Takes a map mu describing several symbolic ranking functions and a model for the corresponding formula and spits out an instantiated ranking function

Takes a relation between program points k, k' and generates constraints that it is bounded when using the (symbolic) rank function for k that is stored in mu.[lex_pos]. Returns a pair of the variable that is equivalent to boundedness in a model and the constraints

Takes a relation between program points k, k' and generates constraints that it is not increasing when using the (symbolic) rank functions for k, k' that are stored in mu.[lex_pos] Returns a pair of the variable that holds the decrease (constrained to be non-negative -- for strict decrease, ask for it to be positive) and the constraints.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.make_decr_and_bnded_constraints(Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,Microsoft.Z3.ArithExpr},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,Microsoft.Z3.ArithExpr},Microsoft.Z3.ArithExpr)
member.summary: Generates the constraints to find a linear rf for rel

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.cp_polyrank
member.summary: a map from cp to whether we're currently finding polyranking fns for this cutpoint. Starts as false, then switches to true as necessary

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.cp_iter_guard
member.summary: a map from cp to the index of the transition that contains the guard

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.cp_current_iter
member.summary: a map from cp to the current number of iterations we're unrolling. Note it starts off at 2 for all cps.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.cp_unrolling
member.summary: a map from cp to a bool describing whether we are currently doing the "unrolling" improvement

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.partial_orders_init_cond
member.summary: map from cp to map. the map is from counters to current partial order for that counter

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.current_counter
member.summary: is the counter identity of the path just found

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.cp_rf_init_cond
member.summary: a map from each cutpoint to a map. the map is from a counter to the index location of the counter's lex checkers

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.cp_init_cond
member.summary: a map from each cp to a bool describing whether we are currently doing the "detect initial condition" improvement

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.cp_attempt_lex
member.summary: a map from each cp to a bool describing whether we are currently doing the lexicographic method (true) or disjunctive method (false)

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.past_lex_options
member.summary: a map from each cp to any lexicographic solutions we found in the past, but did not instrument. Last in first out

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo.partial_orders
member.summary: a map from each cp to the order of relations being used currently

Returns the programs that encodes both input program and the checked property, the error location and a map from cutpoints to the first transition leading to the error location (this is where the rfs are later added in)

Add transitions that ensure the fairness constraint (which may well be None) to the program p, between nodes ret_true_node/ret_false_node and end_node_of_subproperty.
This also takes care of assigning the correct value to ret_var, based on whether we are coming from ret_true_node/ret_false_node.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.add_subproperty_conditions``1(Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Tuple{``0,Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula}},``0,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Extracts the precondition for the current subproperty at cutpoint cp, and adds in new checkers for this subproperty between start_node_for_subproperty and ret_true_node (for cases where it holds)/ret_false_node (for cases where it does not hold)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.var_copy_commands``1(Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program,``0)
member.summary: Make new copied variables and assume commands to store a copy Returns a list of assignments of old vars to new (renamed) vars.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.can_unroll(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo,System.Int32)
member.summary: Return true if we can use unrolling technique:

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.unrolling_trans(System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Int32}},Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Performs that transformation that counts how many times we've looped through cp, and only checks for more than some number of iterations
Switches to detecting initial condition for failure_cp

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.init_cond_trans(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,System.Int32,Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Int32}})
member.summary: Performs the transformation that detects the initial condition at cp and separates the checkers according to the initial condition
Deletes the old lex checkers for failure_cp and get ready to start finding lex polyranking functions

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.switch_to_past_lex_RF(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo,System.Int32)
member.summary: Fetches the past lex option at the head of the queue for failure_cp

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.replace_lex_rf_checkers(Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Int32},System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula}})
member.summary: Replaces a list of old lexicographic checkers by a new list

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.delete_lex_checkers(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Int32},Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Program)
member.summary: Takes in the indexes of the transitions that are lexicographic checkers, deletes them, and returns start and end node of the old check transition (so that you can stuff new checkers in there)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.contains_copied_gets_1``1(``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command})
member.summary: Returns true if cmds contains copied_cp:=1

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.contains_copied_lt_1``1(``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command})
member.summary: Returns true if cmds contains assume(copied_cp<1)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.find_failing_cp``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Tuple{``0,Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command,``1}})
member.summary: Find the cutpoint where we failed, by walking the path backwards until we reach the the check "__copied_k >= 1". k is then the failing cutpoint
Return the appropriate type of Refinement or none

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.find_disj_RF(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,System.Int32,Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Tries to find a WF for cycle_rel

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.find_lex_poly_RF(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,System.Int32,Microsoft.Research.T2.Relation.relation,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String},Microsoft.Research.T2.Instrumentation.LexicographicInfo)
member.summary: Tries to find a Lex_Poly_RF

member.name: P:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.tree_counter
member.summary: counter to name the poly tree debug output dot files uniquely

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.make_poly_checkers(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Rankfunction.poly_tree},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Convert a lexicographic polyranking function (i.e. list of trees) and give out the formulae to be put in the checkers in DNF
Tries to find a Lex_WF, when we're doing the init_cond improvement
Tries to find a Lex_WF or Program_Simplification
Takes in some lexicographic solutions and puts them in past_lex_options in case we want to use them later

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.neg_fn_to_arg(Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Converts a rf to the "negative" formula

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.lim_incr_to_arg(Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term,Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Converts a rf and aux_rf to the "rf increases by no more than aux_rf" formula

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.unaff_fn_to_arg(Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Converts a rf to the "unaffected" formula

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.bounded_fn_to_arg(Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Converts a rf to the "bounded below by zero" formula

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.rankfunction_to_argument(Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term,Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Converts the rf and bound to the "decreasing" and "bounded" formulae

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.lex_rankfunction_to_argument(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Term.term},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Converts the RF and bound lists to the triple formula lists

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Lasso.make_lex_formula(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula})
member.summary: Takes the three formulae lists of a lex RF and gives a single formula expressing the lex RF
Return formula characterizing recurrent set (at the beginning of the cycle) Optimistically calling this "synthesize" assuming that someday it will be based on some smart technology

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.RecurrentSets.separate_conditions_and_assignments(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Research.T2.Formula.formula})
member.summary:
Following Sect. 4.2 of "Automated Detection of Non-Termination and NullPointerExceptions for Java Bytecode"
Obtain loop condition from cycle path in terms of variables at the beginning of the cycle

Find state that repeats itself after <count> iterations of <cycle>. Return disjunction of conjunctions, characterizing state before <cycle>, after one iteration of <cycle>, after two iterations of <cycle> etc.

Proves a CTL property, innermost formulas first, using preconditions. The parameter propertyMap represents a list with the first element being the nested CTL property and the second being a seq of locations/pre-conditions pairs. Note that this map is mutated throughout the proof process.
Prepare the program for another prover run, slowly enumerating all different pre-conditions (which are either conjunctive/disjunctive, depending on whether we are doing universal/existential)
Takes a loc->formula list as second arg, groups the formulas by loc and connects them using the first argument
Tries to remove as many transitions as possible from a SCC. Returns a list of used rank functions/bounds.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Research.T2.Absparse.parsedLoc
member.summary: Location in a T2 file (either numerical or a name)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Absparse.prodIdxToNonTerminal(System.Int32)
member.summary: This function maps production indexes returned in syntax errors to strings representing the non terminal that would be produced by that production

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Input.load_t2(Microsoft.Research.T2.Parameters.parameters,System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Load a program in T2 format

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Input.simplify_chains(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Tuple{System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.Research.T2.Programs.Command},System.String}},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.String})
member.summary: replace edges x->y->z with x->z if there are no other edges from/to y.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Research.T2.Input.parse(System.String)
member.summary: Parse from a file, returning a program
